Title: New insights into bsr-d1-mediated broad-spectrum resistance to rice blast

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Reactive oxygen species metabolism / H2O2 scavenging
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydrogen peroxide using electron-donor substrates, thereby scavenging H2O2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immune signaling molecule involved in rice blast resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes H2O2 degradation and reduces blast resistance; overexpression increases susceptibility to Magnaporthe oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; semi-in vivo ChIP-qPCR; RT-qPCR; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; blast lesion assay; fungal biomass quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BSR-D1 directly binds the Perox3 promoter and activates its expression. 2. Perox3 transcript levels are reduced in Bsr-d1 knockout plants. 3. Perox3 overexpression in TP309 increases lesion length and fungal biomass after M. oryzae infection. 4. Perox3 knockout alone shows no obvious phenotype, suggesting redundancy with other peroxidases.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation or editing of Perox3, especially combined with other redundant peroxidase genes or bsr-d1 deployment, may enhance blast resistance by sustaining H2O2-mediated defense signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves; transcript reduced in Bsr-d1 knockout at the three-leaf stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae isolate ZB15 infection; three-leaf stage growth under 28°C, 12 h light/12 h dark
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Perox3 functions negatively in blast resistance, but knockout alone did not alter disease phenotype likely because of functional redundancy with other peroxidases
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced H2O2 scavenging could increase oxidative damage risk, though not directly tested

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2); salicylic acid-related defense state
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: H2O2 and SA mediate immune signaling and blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: H2O2 degradation and indirect regulation of salicylic acid biosynthesis, metabolism, and signal transduction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of peroxidase genes; indirect regulation of SA-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Perox3; previously reported peroxidase genes LOC_Os05g04470; LOC_Os10g39170
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Perox3 expression directly; negatively affects H2O2 accumulation and indirectly suppresses defense-associated SA pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Magnaporthe oryzae infection induces Bsr-d1 expression in susceptible context according to prior work cited
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Perox3 promoter; promoters of LOC_Os05g04470; LOC_Os10g39170 from prior study
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative regulator of H2O2-mediated broad-spectrum blast resistance; its loss mimics resistant bsr-d1 allele action
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of Bsr-d1 enhances resistance and shifts transcriptome toward oxidative stress and SA-related defense states
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout analysis; RNA-seq; GO/KEGG enrichment; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; semi-in vivo ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bsr-d1 knockout alters relatively few genes but strongly enriches redox-related GO terms. 2. BSR-D1 directly binds and activates the peroxidase gene Perox3. 3. BSR-D1 does not directly bind promoters of tested SA biosynthesis/metabolism/signaling genes, indicating indirect regulation. 4. Bsr-d1-mediated resistance is associated with H2O2 signaling upstream of SA-related responses.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: The resistant bsr-d1 allele or Bsr-d1 loss-of-function can be deployed to improve broad-spectrum rice blast resistance, potentially combined with editing of downstream peroxidases
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible oxidative stress costs from reduced H2O2 scavenging were suggested conceptually but not demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in leaves at the three-leaf stage; knockout lines used for transcriptome and RT-qPCR analyses
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae infection; transcriptome sampled at three-leaf stage
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes BSR-D1 control of redox homeostasis and indirect SA pathway regulation rather than direct control of SA genes
